key_themes: Post-rebrand trust erosion after financial mismanagement and leadership issues in late 2025
Price action near all-time lows (~$0.08, down ~99.6% from 2018 ATH) with low market cap and volume
Tokenomics reform including supply cap at 2.1B and burn of future emissions
Technical upgrades (Spring/Savanna consensus, EOS EVM, performance gains) and pivot to Web3 banking/payments
Limited adoption and community fatigue versus newer Layer-1 competitors
summary: EOS (rebranded to Vaulta with 1:1 swap to $A token in 2025) is trading near its all-time lows around $0.084 with a market cap of roughly $134M, reflecting persistent challenges including a sharp loss of confidence after reported financial mismanagement and CEO changes in late 2025.[[1]](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/eos/)[[2]](https://x.com/nanakusamike/status/2045046099652780074) While token supply has been capped, technical upgrades continue, and the project is targeting Web3 banking, payments, and DeFi/gaming, these catalysts have not driven meaningful recovery or adoption in a competitive market. Community votes on CoinMarketCap lean bullish (~78%), but X activity is quiet and dominated by price-update bots showing red daily moves, resulting in overall negative sentiment.[[1]](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/eos/)[[3]](https://eosnetwork.com/)
sources: CoinMarketCap (current stats, 78% community bullish votes, recent surge mentions), ~20 recent X posts (mostly automated price trackers + limited user discussion), 10+ news/articles on rebrand to Vaulta/$A, tokenomics, upgrades, and 2026 price predictions (Bitget, CoinDesk, EOS Network sites, prediction aggregators)
